features: TL-UL CSR read/write access with same-cycle response and unmapped address error handling
CFG register programming for hmac_en, sha_en, endian_swap, and digest_swap
CMD W1S sequencing for hash_start, hash_process, and hash_stop
STATUS register observation for fifo_full, fifo_empty, fifo_depth, and sha_idle
WIPE_SECRET zeroization behavior for key registers and internal secret state
HMAC mode key processing and SHA-256-only mode operation
Message FIFO depth management and backpressure behavior
Multi-block streaming hash flow with partial and full block handling
Digest register readout and byte-swap behavior
Interrupt and alert pin sanity checks
Reset recovery and post-reset register defaults
Invalid configuration handling for hmac_en without sha_en

directed_tests.name: reset_and_csr_smoke
directed_tests.intent: Verify reset values, TL-UL read/write access, reserved-bit behavior, and unmapped address error response.

directed_tests.name: cfg_mode_matrix
directed_tests.intent: Program all valid CFG combinations, confirm invalid hmac_en without sha_en is rejected or safely ignored, and check mode-dependent status transitions.

directed_tests.name: sha256_single_block_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Run SHA-256-only mode on a known short message, issue hash_start and hash_stop, and compare digest against a software reference.

directed_tests.name: hmac_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Run HMAC-SHA256 on a known key/message pair, verify key processing path, and compare final digest against a software reference.

directed_tests.name: streaming_multi_block_hash
directed_tests.intent: Write message data in multiple chunks across FIFO boundaries, use hash_process and hash_stop, and verify correct digest for a multi-block message.

directed_tests.name: partial_block_padding
directed_tests.intent: Exercise finalization with a non-512-bit-aligned message to validate automatic SHA-256 padding and length encoding.

directed_tests.name: fifo_full_backpressure
directed_tests.intent: Fill the 32-entry FIFO to capacity, confirm STATUS fifo_full and tl_i_ready backpressure behavior, then drain and continue operation.

directed_tests.name: wipe_secret_zeroization
directed_tests.intent: Load key material, trigger WIPE_SECRET, and verify key registers and secret-related state are cleared without corrupting non-secret CSRs.

directed_tests.name: digest_swap_endian_swap
directed_tests.intent: Check byte-order transformation effects on input and output by comparing swapped versus non-swapped digest results.

directed_tests.name: reset_during_active_operation
directed_tests.intent: Assert reset while hashing is active and verify the DUT returns to a clean idle state with cleared FIFO and default CSRs.

random_tests.name: tlul_csr_fuzz
random_tests.intent: Randomize legal TL-UL CSR reads and writes across the register map, including reserved bits and repeated accesses, while checking protocol stability and mirrored CSR behavior.

random_tests.name: streaming_message_fuzzer
random_tests.intent: Generate random message lengths, chunk sizes, and command interleavings within legal sequencing to stress FIFO, padding, and digest generation against a Python SHA-256/HMAC model.

random_tests.name: mode_and_swap_random
random_tests.intent: Randomly vary CFG mode bits and endian/digest swap settings across multiple short transactions to maximize branch coverage in mode-dependent datapaths.

risk_areas.area: HMAC key expansion and double-pass sequencing
risk_areas.reason: Most complex control path; errors here can silently produce incorrect digests even when basic SHA-256 mode passes.
Off-by-one and length-encoding bugs are common in SHA-256 finalization, especially for partial blocks and multi-block streams.

risk_areas.area: FIFO depth, backpressure, and command ordering
risk_areas.reason: The 32-entry FIFO and process/stop sequencing can expose deadlocks or dropped words under boundary conditions.

risk_areas.area: CFG invalid combinations and mode gating
risk_areas.reason: Illegal hmac_en without sha_en may lead to undefined internal state if not handled defensively.
Security-sensitive state clearing must cover key registers and internal secret state without disturbing functional CSRs.
Byte-order transforms are easy to mis-implement and can cause mismatches only on specific message patterns.

risk_areas.area: TL-UL unmapped address and reserved-bit handling
risk_areas.reason: Protocol compliance issues are usually localized but important for integration robustness.
